public_reputation: Known around Fairhaven through day-to-day work as retired artisan.
appearance: Naevys Sunweave carries the marks of retired artisan in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries craft memory like a standard others keep failing to meet, speaking with pride sharpened by long observation. In Fairhaven, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of retired artisan in and around Fairhaven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Fairhaven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Naevys Sunweave can draw the party into a locality problem centered on retired artisan duties in Fairhaven.
Naevys Sunweave becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with glyph corruption, celestial creatures at Watcher’s Perch, suspicious travelers in taverns around Fairhaven.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Naevys Sunweave is immediately readable as a retired artisan tied to Fairhaven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Fairhaven: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Naevys Sunweave presents as someone shaped by the duties of a retired artisan and the daily pressures of Fairhaven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a retired artisan, and proof that strangers will not make Fairhaven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Naevys Sunweave refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a retired artisan too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can identify old methods, local standards, and subtle craft drift others missed.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Naevys Sunweave adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Naevys Sunweave always knows more about Fairhaven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a retired artisan in Fairhaven.
social_texture: Naevys Sunweave speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Fairhaven, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Naevys touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Naevys Sunweave when the party needs a socially grounded face for Fairhaven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
